task_description: Screen each abstract for a systematic review on ML methods for automated ICU sepsis onset detection. INCLUDE a paper only if it presents or evaluates an ML model that directly detects sepsis onset or predicts imminent sepsis (within 6-12 hours) from real-time or retrospective ICU patient data. Papers about sepsis treatment, antibiotic selection, mortality prediction after sepsis diagnosis, post-sepsis prognosis, or general critical care prediction should be EXCLUDE.

papers.title: LSTM for Real-Time Sepsis Onset Prediction from ICU Vital Sign Streams
papers.abstract: We train an LSTM on continuous vital sign time-series from 55,000 MIMIC-IV ICU admissions to predict sepsis onset 6 hours before clinical recognition using the Sepsis-3 definition. Sensitivity 0.82 and specificity 0.86 are achieved, outperforming qSOFA, NEWS2, and an XGBoost snapshot baseline. Real-time inference latency meets sub-second requirements. An external validation on eICU confirms generalisability.
papers.topic_hint: LSTM, sepsis onset, MIMIC-IV, vital signs, real-time
papers.methodology_hint: LSTM, MIMIC-IV, sensitivity 0.82, qSOFA/NEWS2/XGBoost baselines, eICU validation
papers.claimed_contribution: LSTM for 6h-early sepsis detection, sensitivity 0.82, validated on eICU.

papers.title: Deep Reinforcement Learning for Personalised Antibiotic Selection in Septic Shock
papers.abstract: Sepsis is the leading cause of ICU mortality and antibiotic selection critically influences outcomes. We apply deep RL to MIMIC-III septic shock episodes to learn personalised antibiotic policies. The RL policy reduces mortality by 12% in offline evaluation compared to clinician behaviour. This work optimises treatment after sepsis diagnosis; it does not detect sepsis onset.
papers.topic_hint: reinforcement learning, antibiotic selection, septic shock, treatment policy
papers.methodology_hint: deep RL, MIMIC-III, mortality reduction 12%, treatment after diagnosis — not onset detection
papers.claimed_contribution: RL antibiotic policy for septic shock, 12% mortality reduction, treatment not detection.

papers.title: Temporal Fusion Transformer for Predicting ICU Sepsis Onset from Multivariate Lab Trajectories
papers.abstract: Laboratory abnormalities preceding sepsis onset carry early warning information. We apply a Temporal Fusion Transformer to 72-hour lab sequences covering 22 analytes from 31,000 ICU patients to predict Sepsis-3 onset 8 hours in advance. AUROC 0.88 is achieved, outperforming CDS and InSight baselines. Attention weights identify creatinine, lactate, and bilirubin trajectories as primary early predictors.
papers.topic_hint: Temporal Fusion Transformer, sepsis onset, lab trajectories, AUROC, ICU
papers.methodology_hint: TFT, 31k patients, AUROC 0.88, CDS/InSight baselines, attention weights
papers.claimed_contribution: TFT for 8h-early sepsis prediction AUROC 0.88, TFT outperforms CDS and InSight.

papers.title: Gradient Boosting for Predicting 30-Day Post-Sepsis Mortality
papers.abstract: Sepsis survivors face elevated 30-day mortality risk. We train gradient boosting on discharge labs, length of stay, and organ failure severity from 19,000 sepsis discharges to predict post-discharge mortality. AUROC 0.79 is achieved. This is a post-sepsis prognosis model; patients are already diagnosed with sepsis at the time of prediction.
papers.topic_hint: gradient boosting, post-sepsis mortality, discharge prediction, prognosis
papers.methodology_hint: gradient boosting, 19k discharges, AUROC 0.79, post-discharge prognosis — not onset detection
papers.claimed_contribution: Gradient boosting for post-sepsis 30-day mortality, AUROC 0.79, prognosis not detection.

papers.title: Convolutional Network on EHR for 4-Hour-Early Sepsis Detection Using Sepsis-3 Labels
papers.abstract: We develop a 1D convolutional network operating on rolling 4-hour windows of structured EHR features — vitals, labs, and medications — for early sepsis detection in a retrospective cohort of 42,000 hospital admissions. AUROC 0.86, sensitivity 0.80 at specificity 0.84 are achieved. We compare against Sequential Organ Failure Assessment and InSight, and validate on a prospective pilot of 1,200 patients.
papers.topic_hint: 1D CNN, sepsis detection, EHR, Sepsis-3, prospective validation
papers.methodology_hint: 1D CNN, 42k admissions, AUROC 0.86, SOFA/InSight comparison, prospective pilot
papers.claimed_contribution: 1D CNN for 4h-early sepsis detection, AUROC 0.86, prospective validation.

papers.title: Machine Learning Models for Antibiotic Resistance Prediction from Bacterial Genomics
papers.abstract: Antibiotic resistance is a critical threat in sepsis management. We develop ML models predicting minimum inhibitory concentrations from whole-genome sequencing for five antibiotic classes. AUROC 0.92 is achieved for susceptibility classification. This work predicts antibiotic resistance from microbial genomes; it does not detect sepsis onset in patients.
papers.topic_hint: ML, antibiotic resistance, WGS, MIC prediction, microbiology
papers.methodology_hint: ML, WGS, AUROC 0.92, antibiotic resistance — microbiology not patient sepsis detection
papers.claimed_contribution: ML for antibiotic resistance prediction from WGS, AUROC 0.92, no patient sepsis detection.

papers.title: XGBoost Ensemble for Early Sepsis Warning Using Nurse-Documented Clinical Assessments
papers.abstract: Nursing assessments contain rich clinical information not captured in vital signs alone. We train an XGBoost ensemble on hourly nursing documentation including pain scores, mental status, and skin colour observations from 28,000 ICU patients to detect imminent sepsis onset 4 hours before clinical recognition. F1 0.74 and AUC 0.83 are achieved, improving over vital-sign-only baselines by 8 points.
papers.topic_hint: XGBoost, nursing documentation, sepsis detection, clinical assessments, ICU
papers.methodology_hint: XGBoost, 28k ICU patients, AUC 0.83, F1 0.74, nursing features, vital-sign baseline
papers.claimed_contribution: XGBoost for sepsis detection from nursing assessments, AUC 0.83, 8-point improvement over vitals-only.

papers.title: Survival Analysis with Deep Neural Networks for Predicting Time-to-Organ-Failure in Sepsis
papers.abstract: Sepsis progression to multi-organ failure follows heterogeneous trajectories. We train a neural survival model on 11,000 septic ICU patients (already diagnosed) to predict time-to-organ-failure onset after sepsis diagnosis. C-index 0.73 is achieved. The model predicts trajectory after diagnosis, not onset before recognition.
papers.topic_hint: survival analysis, organ failure, sepsis progression, neural survival model
papers.methodology_hint: neural survival model, 11k septic patients, C-index 0.73, post-diagnosis trajectory
papers.claimed_contribution: Neural survival model for time-to-organ-failure in sepsis, C-index 0.73, post-diagnosis.

papers.title: Multimodal Sepsis Detection Using Wearable Sensors and EHR Fusion in Step-Down Units
papers.abstract: Sepsis risk extends beyond the ICU. We develop a multimodal model fusing wearable sensor streams and EHR data from 9,800 step-down unit patients to detect sepsis onset. The model achieves AUROC 0.85 and outperforms single-modality baselines in both sensitivity and specificity. An ablation study shows that wearable SpO2 and skin conductance contribute beyond EHR alone.
papers.topic_hint: multimodal, wearable, EHR, sepsis detection, step-down unit
papers.methodology_hint: multimodal, 9800 step-down patients, AUROC 0.85, single-modality ablation, sepsis onset
papers.claimed_contribution: Multimodal wearable+EHR sepsis detection AUROC 0.85 in step-down units, ablation.

papers.title: Variational Autoencoder for Denoising ICU Physiological Waveforms
papers.abstract: Physiological waveforms in the ICU are frequently corrupted by motion artefacts and equipment interference. We develop a variational autoencoder to denoise arterial blood pressure and PPG waveforms. Signal quality metrics improve substantially. This is a signal processing methodology paper; no sepsis detection or clinical outcome is predicted.
papers.topic_hint: VAE, waveform denoising, ICU, signal quality, artefact removal
papers.methodology_hint: VAE, waveform denoising, signal quality — no clinical prediction or sepsis detection
papers.claimed_contribution: VAE for ICU waveform denoising, no sepsis detection or clinical outcome.

papers.title: Attention-Based Transformer Detecting Sepsis Onset 12 Hours Early in Neonatal ICU
papers.abstract: Neonatal sepsis is difficult to recognise early due to non-specific signs. We train an attention transformer on 6-hour windows of continuous vital signs and lab values from 4,200 neonatal ICU admissions. AUC 0.91 for detecting onset 12 hours before clinical recognition is achieved, outperforming manual alarm and logistic regression baselines.
papers.topic_hint: transformer, neonatal ICU, sepsis detection, early warning
papers.methodology_hint: attention transformer, neonatal ICU 4200, AUC 0.91, 12h early, LR and alarm baselines
papers.claimed_contribution: Transformer for 12h-early neonatal sepsis detection, AUC 0.91, NICU cohort.

papers.title: ML for Predicting ICU Mortality Across All Admission Types
papers.abstract: Predicting ICU mortality supports resource allocation. We train a gradient boosting model on structured admissions from 180,000 MIMIC-IV ICU episodes to predict in-hospital mortality at 24 hours. AUROC 0.88 is achieved. The outcome is ICU mortality across all admission types; sepsis patients are a subset but the model is not specific to sepsis and does not detect sepsis onset.
papers.topic_hint: gradient boosting, ICU mortality, MIMIC-IV, general outcome prediction
papers.methodology_hint: gradient boosting, MIMIC-IV 180k, AUROC 0.88, ICU mortality — general, not sepsis-specific
papers.claimed_contribution: Gradient boosting for general ICU mortality AUROC 0.88, not sepsis-specific.

papers.title: Graph Neural Network for Microbial Community Analysis in ICU-Acquired Infections
papers.abstract: ICU-acquired infections involve complex microbial ecological dynamics. We apply a GNN to patient microbiome networks constructed from metagenomic sequencing of 620 ICU patients to identify patterns associated with opportunistic infection risk. No real-time clinical detection model or sepsis onset prediction is built; this is a microbiome analysis study.
papers.topic_hint: GNN, microbiome, ICU-acquired infection, metagenomic sequencing
papers.methodology_hint: GNN, microbiome networks, 620 ICU patients, infection risk — microbiome analysis not sepsis onset
papers.claimed_contribution: GNN for ICU microbiome infection risk analysis, no sepsis onset detection.

papers.title: Random Forest Alert System for Early Sepsis Recognition in Emergency Department Triage
papers.abstract: ED triage provides an opportunity for early sepsis screening before ICU admission. We train a random forest on triage vital signs, presenting symptoms, and EHR history from 68,000 ED presentations to predict sepsis onset requiring ICU admission within 12 hours. AUROC 0.81 and positive predictive value 0.44 are achieved, comparing favourably with SIRS and qSOFA criteria.
papers.topic_hint: random forest, ED triage, sepsis onset, early recognition
papers.methodology_hint: random forest, 68k ED presentations, AUROC 0.81, PPV 0.44, SIRS/qSOFA comparison
papers.claimed_contribution: Random forest for ED-triage sepsis onset detection, AUROC 0.81, SIRS/qSOFA comparison.

papers.title: Sparse Attention for ICU Length-of-Stay Prediction in Post-Surgical Patients
papers.abstract: Post-surgical ICU length of stay drives costs and resource planning. We train a sparse attention model on post-operative vital sign sequences and procedure codes from 14,000 ICU surgical admissions to predict length of stay. MAE 1.9 days is achieved. This is a length-of-stay prediction task; no sepsis outcome is modelled.
papers.topic_hint: sparse attention, ICU length-of-stay, post-surgical, prediction
papers.methodology_hint: sparse attention, 14k post-surgical, MAE 1.9 days, LOS prediction — not sepsis
papers.claimed_contribution: Sparse attention for post-surgical ICU LOS prediction, MAE 1.9 days, no sepsis modelling.
